group.name: Write-time vs validate-time asymmetry (a write is accepted but is not validate-clean; corruption caught only post-hoc)

statement: Write-time edge guard accepts dangling / cross-alias / cycle-closing endpoints (FGAP-093)
action: Extend the write-time edge validator to reject an edge whose offending endpoint doesn't exist (dangling/cross-alias) and edges that close a cycle, so a write-accepted edge is always validate-clean — not merely caught post-hoc by validateContext. (TASK-062 already added only the kind/category half.)

in: the write-time edge guard (kind-validator shipped via completed TASK-062, endpoint/cycle half open)

verify: FGAP-093 status flips off `identified` with a closure/VER record; a write attempting a dangling-endpoint or cycle-closing edge is rejected at write time (not only by a later validateContext pass).

group.name: Write-time vs validate-time asymmetry (a write is accepted but is not validate-clean; corruption caught only post-hoc)

statement: layer-plans schema nests id-bearing arrays instead of top-level entities + edges (issue-002)
action: Restructure the layer-plans schema so `plans.layers` / `plans.migration_phases` become top-level entities plus membership edges, eliminating the nested id-bearing arrays that `findNestedIdBearingArrays` flags on every scan.

in: packages/pi-context/samples/schemas/layer-plans.schema.json (both retain the nesting per apr-may report)

verify: `findNestedIdBearingArrays` scan no longer emits the 2 layer-plans warnings; issue-002 moves to closed/resolved.

group.name: No recovery path once state is already invalid (validate-before-mutate deadlocks)

statement: Config repair impossible once stored config is invalid (validate-before-mutate deadlock) (FGAP-096)
action: Add a sanctioned CLI path that repairs an already-invalid `.context/config` without requiring pre-existing validity, breaking the validate-before-repair deadlock (its two sibling gaps FGAP-095 and FGAP-097 from the same filing are already closed).
verify: A CLI command can restore a corrupt/invalid stored config to validity without direct-editing the file; FGAP-096 closes with a VER record.

group.name: Silent false status signals + install/lifecycle bookkeeping gaps (breakage that lies rather than throws; fresh-install/status-enum omissions)

statement: No op materializes a newly-declared block_kind's data file; false `not-installed` bootstrap signal (FGAP-065)
action: Make catalog-added block kinds materialize their data file into an installed substrate so context-bootstrap-state stops reporting a fully-usable substrate as `not-installed` on a dangling block_kind declaration.

out: related July residual FGAP-140 is a distinct atom — ATOM-KB-05 (KB-05)

verify: Adding a catalog block kind no longer flips a usable substrate to `not-installed`; the newly-declared block's data file is materialized; FGAP-065 closes.

group.name: Silent false status signals + install/lifecycle bookkeeping gaps (breakage that lies rather than throws; fresh-install/status-enum omissions)

statement: Fresh install never seeds block-schema migration declarations from the catalog (FGAP-140)
action: Add block-schema migration-declaration seeding on fresh install (a `seedCatalogBlock…`-style function) so `installContext`/`checkStatus` seed catalog block-schema migration decls, not only config migrations — mirroring the already-fixed update-time twin FGAP-141.
verify: A fresh `.context` install lands block schemas (e.g. `session-notes`/`research`) with their migration decls and `context-validate` returns 0 errors; a block-schema seeding function exists; FGAP-140 closes.

group.name: Silent false status signals + install/lifecycle bookkeeping gaps (breakage that lies rather than throws; fresh-install/status-enum omissions)

statement: No lifecycle status enum carries a `paused` value (FGAP-103)
action: Add a `paused` value to the lifecycle status enum(s) so a deliberately-suspended phase/task can be represented instead of being mapped `paused→blocked` or presented as active work.
verify: `grep '"paused"'` over the status schemas now matches; a paused arc renders as paused (not active/blocked); FGAP-103 closes.

group.name: Surface-parity holes (a capability exists as CLI op / registry entry but is unreachable from the human `/context` surface or from non-interactive contexts)

statement: `/context` human slash surface omits `update` + 4 operator ops; parity-check blind to it (FGAP-135)
action: Register `update` (and `resolve-conflict`, `resolve-blocked`, `context-validate-relations`, `reconcile`) in `CONTEXT_SUBCOMMANDS`, and extend the parity check to cover the human `/context` surface (not only op↔CLI), so the reconcile/update/conflict ops are reachable in-pi.
verify: The five ops appear in `CONTEXT_SUBCOMMANDS` and are invokable via `/context`; the parity check now fails when a CLI op lacks a human-surface subcommand; FGAP-135 closes.

group.name: Surface-parity holes (a capability exists as CLI op / registry entry but is unreachable from the human `/context` surface or from non-interactive contexts)

verify: A work order can invoke the previously pi-only auth-gated tools non-interactively; FGAP-126 closes.

group.name: Doc-vs-implementation drift that fails at runtime (advertised names the code will reject)

verify: Every filter named in the README resolves in the registry (no runtime throw), or the README lists only implemented filters; the two counts match; FGAP-131 closes.

group.name: Install-time materialization / UX gaps

group.name: Op-boundary contract divergence — preview reports what live refuses; params pass unvalidated

statement: Op run() boundary has no input-contract enforcement — unknown/misspelled params are silently accepted on every op (the CLI rejects at parse; in-pi tool dispatch does not), so `dryrun` for `dryRun` runs the LIVE write the caller meant to preview — self-documented in code, drafted as a filing, not filed and not fixed at HEAD
action: File the drafted class-level gap (payload filings/op-param-silent-acceptance.json) once the user authorizes, then enforce the input contract at the op run() boundary (schema-level additionalProperties rejection or a registerAll-level unknown-key check) instead of per-op hand-rolled guards like rejectBlockNarrowing.

in: packages/pi-context/src/ops-registry.ts registerAll (:3089-3118) and op param schemas
out: the CLI parse layer (already rejects unknown flags, cli.ts:332)

verify: An op invocation carrying an undeclared param (e.g. `dryrun`) is refused with an unknown-parameter error instead of silently running live/unfiltered; the gap exists in framework-gaps with a closure record.

group.name: Write-time content-gate retroactivity — item-grain scoping wedges closure and engine loops

statement: Item-grain diff-scoping makes every write-time rhetorical-gate extension retroactive: a status-only mutation re-asserts the whole item against the CURRENT rule set, wedging closure transitions (complete-task on TASK-131 refused, closure atom left half-applied) and engine loops on grandfathered filed-valid text (FGAP-152)
action: Fix per FGAP-152's proposed resolution: value-grain diff-scoping — thread the PRIOR on-disk item alongside the merged item at all three threading sites (block-api.ts:1537/1658/1849) and skip any field or bare-string-array element whose value deep-equals the prior, so enforcement fires on exactly the text the write authors.

out: append path (no prior; current behavior preserved)

verify: FGAP-152 status flips off `identified` with a closure record; a status-only flip on an item carrying pre-extension text succeeds while editing a violating field still trips the gate; the FGAP-152 reproduction case passes.
